title: lucilia burkartii
side_effects: Lucilia burkartii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause skin lesions, blisters, and ulcers, which can become infected if left untreated. Additionally, improper use of Lucilia burkartii may trigger anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction, in rare cases.
